title: Dawn Square: Smoke and Applause
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, dawn in city square, soot in air, civilians cheering and grieving, guards tired, cinematic, no text
description:
Dawn finds the city standing.

Some cheer because they must.

Some weep because they can.

Your name moves through the crowd like a rumor becoming fact.

choices.label: Go to the council for aftermath orders.
Visit the Armourers’ Guild for veteran crafting access.
Visit the Scribes’ Conclave for veteran spell access.

title: Council Afterbattle: The Next Knife
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, council chamber with scorched maps and wax seals, morning light, cinematic, no text
description:
The council speaks in numbers:

Casualties.
Supplies.
Repair time.

Vayne speaks in outcomes:

"They retreated. They will return—unless we make retreat expensive."

talk.text: Politics begins the moment the swords stop.

choices.label: Depart the city to pursue the enemy retreat.

title: Barracks Report: Ash Ledger
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, barracks desk with casualty lists, soot-stained captain, cinematic, no text
description:
Captain Harl’s desk is a battlefield of paper.

A city can be saved and still wounded.

He looks up as you arrive.

talk.text: Harl speaks plainly: consequences are a kind of enemy.

title: Armourers’ Guild: Veteran Work
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, busy forge with armor pieces and guildmaster, sparks, cinematic, no text
description:
The guild doesn’t applaud.

It measures.

Hammer marks. Stitch lines. Fit.

This is where veterans become harder to kill.

talk.text: Orrow judges you with a craftsman’s eyes.

title: Scribes’ Conclave: Ink That Bites
imagePrompt: fantasy illustration, inked linework + painterly color, scribe hall with scroll racks and glowing runes, quiet intensity, cinematic, no text
description:
The air smells like ink and burnt lavender.

Quiet people doing loud work.

Luvara doesn’t waste words.
